stdout:
OBSERVATIONS:
- Image 1, category 2 (texture): the mottled cloudy texture is very low-frequency and smooth — soft blotchy gradients with no fine-grained micro-relief or rolled-glass ripple visible even at close crop; it reads like a soft blurred/painted noise field rather than glass surface grain.
- Image 1, category 6 (optics): the whole frame including the background scribbles has uniform soft blur with no distinguishable focal plane — the black squiggly marks (grease pencil/marker lines) are blurred at the same softness as the glass texture behind them, suggesting a shallow-DOF or defocus applied uniformly rather than true camera focus falloff.
- Image 1, category 5 (lighting): the bright hotspot in the upper-left corner falls off very smoothly and symmetrically with no directional streaking or environment shape — consistent with either but notably featureless, a single soft radial gradient.
- Image 2, category 2 (texture): dense, high-frequency vertical waviness (rolling lines) with irregular spacing and amplitude, plus scattered small bright specks/bubbles (visible as tiny white dots and one ring-shaped inclusion near center-right) — consistent with real rolled/streaky glass and embedded bubbles.
- Image 2, category 6 (noise/optics): fine sensor-level grain is visible in the dark region at left, and the small bubble inclusions have subtle bright specular pinpoints consistent with real light refracting through embedded glass defects.
- Image 2, category 1 (reflections): the dark-to-light transition band shows soft directional sheen consistent with a physical light source behind/beside the sheet, with localized brighter streaks tracking the glass ripples rather than a flat gradient.

VERDICT: Image 1 synthetic -- its texture is an overly smooth, low-frequency blotchy field lacking the fine rolled-glass grain, embedded bubbles, or sensor noise seen in image 2, and its blur/lighting look like a uniform post-process rather than optical falloff from a real lens and light source.
